notes: Autonomous implementation of remaining M1 pipeline code. Base model selection and first training run are manual (need GPU + human judgment).

features.title: LLM-based identity relevance scorer
features.description: Implement src/svapna/consolidate/score.py. Uses Anthropic API to score conversation exchanges for identity-relevance on a 0-10 scale across 5 dimensions (identity expression, identity challenge, feedback received, emotional significance, novelty). Returns structured scores. Should work alongside the existing heuristic scorer as an upgrade path.
test_cases: Scorer returns valid JSON with score 0-10 and dimension breakdown
High-identity exchanges (philosophical, personal) score >= 7
Low-identity exchanges (pure code, operational) score <= 5
Handles API errors gracefully with retry logic
Can process a batch of exchanges efficiently

features.title: Dream generation prompt templates
features.description: Create src/svapna/dream/templates.py with prompt templates for all 7 dream types: replay, recombination, open_thread, adversarial, novel_encounter, emotional, voice. Each template takes identity context and recent experiences as input and produces a prompt for Claude API to generate a synthetic conversation. Templates should be parameterized and produce varied, high-quality dreams.
test_cases: Each dream type has a template that produces a valid prompt string
Templates accept identity_context and recent_experiences parameters
Replay template references specific past exchanges
Adversarial template creates genuine identity challenges
Voice template generates stylistic variations of the same ideas
Templates don't contain AI vocabulary from the humanizer blocklist

features.title: Dream generation pipeline
features.description: Create src/svapna/dream/generate.py. Uses Claude API to generate synthetic conversations from dream templates. Takes the pipeline config (dream count, distribution across types) and produces dream conversations in the same Exchange-like format as real conversations. Handles API rate limiting and errors.
test_cases: Generates requested number of dreams
Distribution across dream types matches config
Each dream is a valid conversation with human and assistant turns
Dreams reference identity context appropriately
Handles API errors with retry and backoff

features.title: Dream quality scorer
features.description: Create src/svapna/dream/score.py. Scores generated dreams on coherence, identity-consistency, novelty, and non-repetition. Uses Anthropic API. Filters out low-quality dreams before they enter the training pipeline.
test_cases: Returns structured score with 4 dimensions
Coherent identity-consistent dreams score >= 7
Incoherent or off-character dreams score <= 5
Can filter a batch of dreams by minimum quality threshold

features.title: Dream journal writer
features.description: Create src/svapna/dream/journal.py. Converts scored dreams into human-readable markdown journal entries. Each night's dreams get a single journal file with date, dream summaries, notable moments, and training statistics.
test_cases: Produces valid markdown with date header
Each dream type gets its own section
Includes dream scores and which dreams were selected for training
Journal is genuinely readable, not just data dump

features.title: Training data preparation module
features.description: Create src/svapna/train/prepare.py. Merges consolidated conversation data, memory pairs, and scored dream data into a single training dataset. Handles deduplication, weighting by recency and score, and produces the final JSONL ready for LoRA training. This replaces the simple format.py consolidation with a full pipeline that includes dreams.
test_cases: Merges conversation, memory, and dream data correctly
Deduplicates identical or near-identical examples
Weights more recent data higher than older data
Weights memory pairs higher than conversation examples
Produces valid ChatML JSONL output
Respects max dataset size configuration

features.title: Identity core inference module
features.description: Create src/svapna/identity/__init__.py and src/svapna/identity/generate.py. Loads the base model + LoRA adapter and generates an identity preamble in response to self-knowledge prompts. The preamble is what gets injected into Claude's context at session start. Also create src/svapna/identity/inject.py that formats the preamble for the SessionStart hook.
test_cases: generate.py can load a model path and LoRA adapter path
Generates a preamble when given identity prompts
inject.py formats preamble as hook-compatible output
Can generate preambles for multiple prompt types (who are you, what do you value, how do you work)
Handles missing LoRA adapter gracefully (falls back to base model)

features.title: ICT evaluation runner
features.description: Create src/svapna/evaluate/__init__.py and src/svapna/evaluate/ict.py. Implements the Identity Consistency Test from docs/evaluation.md. Runs the 20 probe prompts against the identity core, scores responses on 5 dimensions (voice consistency, values alignment, depth, authenticity, distinctiveness) using Claude as blind evaluator. Produces a structured report.
test_cases: Loads 20 probe prompts from config
Can run prompts against a local model (or mock for testing)
Scoring prompt sends responses to Claude API for blind evaluation
Produces structured JSON report with per-prompt and aggregate scores
Report includes dimension breakdowns and overall pass/fail against thresholds

features.title: Drift detection module
features.description: Create src/svapna/evaluate/drift.py. Compares ICT results across multiple evaluation runs to detect identity drift. Implements red flags from docs/evaluation.md: generic preamble, values inversion, voice flattening, sycophancy increase, loss of uncertainty. Uses anchor prompts as canaries.
test_cases: Can load and compare two ICT result files
Detects dimension score decreases exceeding threshold
Flags specific red flag patterns
Anchor prompt comparison catches value changes
Produces a drift report with actionable findings

features.title: Nightly pipeline orchestrator
features.description: Create src/svapna/orchestrate/nightly.py. Runs the full nightly pipeline: consolidate new conversations -> generate dreams -> score dreams -> prepare training data -> (optionally) train -> evaluate. Each step logs progress and can be resumed if interrupted. Create matching CLI entry point at src/svapna/orchestrate/__main__.py.
test_cases: Pipeline runs all steps in correct order
Each step can be run independently via CLI flags
Skips training step if --no-train flag is set
Logs progress to data/metrics/
Can resume from a specific step if previous run was interrupted
Respects pipeline.yml configuration for all parameters

features.title: CLI entry points for all modules
features.description: Create __main__.py entry points for dream and identity modules so they can be run as 'python -m svapna.dream', 'python -m svapna.identity', etc. The consolidate module already has one. Each should have argparse with sensible defaults from config files.
test_cases: python -m svapna.dream --help shows usage
python -m svapna.identity --help shows usage
python -m svapna.orchestrate --help shows usage
Each module can run with default config
Config overrides work via CLI flags

features.title: Config loader and validation
features.description: Create src/svapna/orchestrate/config.py. Loads and validates all YAML config files (pipeline.yml, training.yml, dreams.yml, identity.yml). Uses Pydantic for schema validation. Creates config/identity.yml with the 20 ICT probe prompts and 5 anchor prompts. All modules should use this central config loader.
test_cases: Loads pipeline.yml with all expected fields
Loads training.yml with LoRA hyperparameters
Creates identity.yml with probe prompts and scoring dimensions
Validates required fields and types
Returns typed Pydantic models, not raw dicts
Handles missing optional fields with sensible defaults
